topic: A person discovers the tree they planted as a child has been cut down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The scent of damp earth and pine needles hit Elias like a physical blow. He stood at the edge of the clearing, his breath catching in shallow, ragged gasps. Where a sturdy, young oak had stood just a few years ago, now there was only a raw, gaping wound in the landscape. His small, earnest efforts, the watering, the careful weeding—all erased in a single, brutal sweep of machinery. He sank to his knees, the grit of the disturbed soil scratching his skin. His hands trembled, not with anger, but with a deep, sickening hollowness. He kept staring at the stumps, his eyes wide and unfocused, as if watching a phantom limb disappear. A low, involuntary sound escaped his throat, a strangled whisper that seemed too large for his chest.

[story 2]

“No. No, this isn't right.” Sarah walked the perimeter of the property, her footsteps heavy and deliberate on the overgrown path. Each step brought a fresh wave of nausea. The space where the sapling had been—a hopeful green promise—was now just bare, scarred earth. She ran a hand over her jaw, feeling the tightness constrict beneath her skin. She walked back to the center, her gaze fixed on the ragged edges of the cut. Her knuckles whitened as she clenched her fists. She didn't cry; the tears wouldn't come. Instead, a cold, sharp pressure built behind her eyes, making the world feel too loud, too sharp. She turned abruptly, needing distance, and walked toward the house, her movements jerky, as if trying to outrun the sight.

[story 3]

He circled the perimeter three times, his pace accelerating until his lungs burned. Every rustle in the nearby brush sounded like a deliberate mockery. The silence of the clearing was oppressive, thick with the memory of what had been. Elias stopped near the base of the largest remaining stump. He didn't touch it, but he leaned in close, his face contorted in a silent grimace. His jaw worked rhythmically, a repetitive motion that seemed to shake his entire frame. He looked down at his own hands, noticing the slight tremor that persisted even after he forced them still. A hot flush spread across his neck and cheeks, and he swallowed hard, the effort tasting like ash. He just stood there, rooted to the spot, absorbing the void left behind.
